fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: In order to support emergency response operations, enhancing and clearly marking the single access point could facilitate the quick entry of emergency vehicles onto the property. Maintaining unobstructed routes through the front driveway may assist with swift movement to and from the main building. Regularly trimming and managing the landscaped area could support better visibility and easier navigation for emergency responders. Ensuring that open green space remains clear and accessible might offer additional staging areas or alternate routes for equipment and personnel. Considering these measures may enhance overall responder access, movement, and operational efficiency during emergencies.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Open Terrain; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Long Driveway; Defensible Space; Open Fuel Break
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by ensuring separation between scattered trees and shrubs, particularly near the structure, could improve fuel reduction and mitigate wildfire exposure. Prioritizing the management of vegetation adjacent to the structure and maintaining adequate spacing could further reduce fuel continuity and potential exposure. The open terrain and existing fuel breaks present an opportunity to enhance separation and support suppression efforts. Regular maintenance of the long driveway by managing vegetation along its length would enhance firefighter access and bolster suppression capabilities. Preserving the existing open fuel breaks and defensible spaces, along with consistent management of vegetation, may support long-term wildfire resilience and improve property readiness.
